title: CIOs on AI: Measuring Impact Beyond Traditional Metrics - LinkedIn
search_path_label: A 级媒体 / GDELT 路径
full_text:
As Steve Rosenbush writes in the Wall Street Journal, "While AI is still rapidly evolving, CIOs are recognizing that traditional ways of recognizing gains from the technology aren’t cutting it."
As Steve Rosenbush writes in the Wall Street Journal, "While AI is still rapidly evolving, CIOs are recognizing that traditional ways of recognizing gains from the technology aren’t cutting it."
I was interested to read takeaways from CIOs who are focused on innovation, as well as experimentation and learning, when implementing AI across their organizations.
I'm constantly thinking about measuring the impact of technology, whether it's from an initial AI pilot or a scaled transformation across a function like customer service. Once we have the power of scale, the ways we measure AI's value become increasingly complex. We can't just measure hours saved or value created, but rather broader impact across the organization in terms of culture shift and employee satisfaction.
It was a pleasure to speak with Steve earlier this year on this topic and since we spoke, this space has continued to evolve. You can read more in the Wall Street Journal: https://lnkd.in/gA8k6wTm
